macro_segments.title: Woman's Claims of Unfitness: Belief and Disbelief in the Mishnah

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah: Woman Claiming Rape or Adultery
First Position (Mishnah Rishona): Woman is Believed and Gets Ketubah
Second Position (Mishnah Acharona): Woman is Not Believed Due to Fear of Ulterior Motive
Rambam's Middle Position: Woman is Believed but Without Ketubah, Husband Forced to Divorce

macro_segments.title: Rav Sheshet vs. Rava: The Terumah Question and Doubt

micro_segments.title: The Question: Can She Continue Eating Terumah if Not Believed?
Rav Sheshet's Position: She Eats Terumah to Avoid Public Stigma
Concern of Shelo Totzi La'az: Not Raising Questions About Children's Status
Rava's Position: She Should Not Eat Terumah; Assumes Chulin Food
Rava's Qualification of Rav Sheshet: Doubt Persists After Widowing or Divorce

macro_segments.title: Deeper Analysis: Standards of Belief and Personal Status

micro_segments.title: Comparison to Meat and Grain: Cheskas Tahara and Eid Echad Ba'issurin
The Threshold Question: What Standard of Proof is Needed for Personal Status?
Logical vs. Takana: Did the Rabbis Override Default Belief or Reassess Believability?

macro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Question: Wife of Kohen Who Was Raped—Does She Receive Ketubah?

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa Quizzes: If Ones to Kohen is Like Willful Adultery to Yisrael, No Ketubah?
Counter-Argument: Woman Can Say 'I Am Fit'—Husband's Status is the Problem
Resolution: No-Fault Divorce with Ketubah Because Fault Lies with Kohen Status

macro_segments.title: New Question: Woman Claims Husband Divorced Her (Gerashtani)

micro_segments.title: The Case: Woman Says Divorced Without Producing Get
Rav Hamnuna's Position: Chazaka Isha Lo Meiza Paneha—Woman Believed in Direct Statement
Logic: Husband Knows Truth; Woman Unlikely to Lie to His Face

macro_segments.title: Rava's Counter: Default Is Not to Believe; Specific Reasons Needed

micro_segments.title: Rava's Reading of Mishnah Rishona: Shame Prevents Lying in Specific Cases
Rava's Position on Gerashtani: Motivation to Exit Marriage Overcomes Chazaka
Implication: Even in Mishnah Rishona, When We Don't Believe, We Don't Believe Absolutely

macro_segments.title: Gemara Tests Both Positions with Cases from the Mishnah

micro_segments.title: Rav Mesharshya's Challenge to Rava: Hashamayim Beini U'Veinecha Case
Woman's Claim About Fertility and Rava's Response: She Must Name Specific Defect
Rava's Reassertion: Even Explicit Claims Need Special Reasons for Belief
Counter-Test: In Mishnah Acharona, Would Woman Be Believed About Hashamayim?
Rava's Final Position: She Convinces Herself Husband Doesn't Know, So She Lies
Standard Practice: We Follow Rav Hamnuna's Chazaka Against Rava
Ran's Derivation: Woman Believed When Claiming Husband Refuses Relations
Halakhic Categories by Ran: Wife's Claim Affects Ketubah Entitlement
Ran's Caveat: Mentioning Money Weakens Claim; Focus on Get Strengthens It

macro_segments.title: Story One: Woman's Daily Intimacy Gesture and Husband's Accusation

micro_segments.title: Setup: Woman Daily Washes Husband's Hands After Relations
Husband's Surprise: 'We Didn't Have Relations Today; Why Wash My Hands?'
Wife's Explanation: 'Must Have Been the Fragrance Merchants—One Came to Me in the Dark'
Rav Nachman's Ruling: Don't Pay Attention to Her; She Wants Divorce

macro_segments.title: Story Two: Man Hidden in House and Woman's Claim of Mistaken Identity

micro_segments.title: Setup: Woman Not in Good Mood; Claims Recent Relations Were Painful
Husband's Response: 'We Didn't Have Relations Today'
Wife's Explanation: 'Must Be the Fragrance Merchants Who Were Here Today'
Rav Nachman Repeats: Don't Pay Attention; She Wants Another Man

macro_segments.title: Story Three: Adulterer Hiding and Circumstantial Evidence

micro_segments.title: Setup: Man Caught in Closed Room with Married Woman Runs Away
Rava's Logic: If They Committed Adultery, He Would Have Hidden Better
Ran's Clarification: Circumstantial Evidence Alone Doesn't Forbid Wife
Reassurance Function: Rabbis Calm Husband's Fears While Maintaining Halakhic Clarity

macro_segments.title: Story Four: Adulterer in Closet Saves Husband's Life

micro_segments.title: Setup: Adulterer Hiding as Husband Arrives; Cress with Snake-Bite on Table
Crisis: Husband About to Eat Poisoned Cress; Adulterer Reveals Himself to Warn
Rava's Conclusion: If He Committed Adultery, He'd Want Husband Dead; He Didn't Warn Thus He Didn't Act
Counter-Argument Rejected: Adulterer Might Want Secret Affair, Not Marriage

macro_segments.title: Synthesis: Chazal's Method in Resolving Marital Crises and Maintaining Shalom Bayis

micro_segments.title: Context: Stories Conclude a Perek on Vows and Marital Harmony
Dual Method: Formal Disbelief Combined with Circumstantial Reassurance
Goal: Reducing Marital Discord While Maintaining Halakhic Integrity

topical_tags.term: Shema Eineyha Natna Be'Acher (Motivation for Adultery)
Rambam on Woman's Claim of Inability to Cohabitate
